findings.one_line: The theorem overstates the encoded rate premise: `ass:nuisance-estimator-rate` is uniform over `W_n`, but the statement/proof also extend the same class-constant envelope to `W_n^reg` by adding an extra pointwise premise not present in the node.

The proof discharges the node by introducing a fold partition and foldwise nuisance fits `(hat g_{1,m}, hat g_{0,m}, hat e_m)` that are not carried by the declared symbols or by `def:crossfit-estimator`, so the cross-fit argument uses undeclared structure.

The claimed constants 'depending only on the class constants' are not derivable from `W_n` as encoded, because `ass:tail-shell` supplies shell constants existentially per law; the proof yields law-specific comparison constants, not a class-uniform `U_n \asymp R_n` statement.

Parts (a) and (b) upgrade the estimator-side `O_P(U_n(\lambda))` result into a class-uniform upper frontier of order `R_n^*`, but that upgrade is not discharged because it relies on the missing class-uniform comparison and on an upper-bound route that is not encoded as a single minimax-valid estimator node.

The headline claims a sharp matched minimax theorem on a sub-regime, while the discharged core supports only a parametric lower floor plus estimator-side achievability and leaves the exact weak-overlap minimax rate open.
